title: Teaching Assistant – Introduction to Psychology
description: Assist the lead instructor in PSY 2012 by grading exams, holding office hours, leading weekly discussion sections of 25 students, and proctoring midterm and final exams. Opportunity to guest-lecture once per semester.
qualifications: Must have completed PSY 2012 with a B+ or higher. Strong communication skills and prior tutoring experience preferred.

title: Teaching Assistant – Calculus I
description: Support MAC 2311 by running two recitation sections per week, grading homework sets, and staffing the Math Learning Center for 4 hours weekly. Collaborate with course coordinator on exam preparation.
qualifications: Completion of Calculus III with a B or higher. Experience with LaTeX typesetting is a plus.

title: Teaching Assistant – Organic Chemistry I
description: Lead three weekly recitation sessions for CHM 2210, hold office hours, proctor exams, and assist with grading. Work closely with the professor to develop practice problem sets.
qualifications: Completed Organic Chemistry II with a grade of A-. Must be a graduate student in Chemistry or Biochemistry.

title: Teaching Assistant – Introduction to Sociology
description: Facilitate two discussion sections of 30 students each, grade essays and short-answer exams, maintain the course Canvas page, and meet weekly with the lead instructor to coordinate lesson plans.
qualifications: Enrolled in a Sociology graduate program. Familiarity with Canvas LMS required.

title: Teaching Assistant – Data Structures and Algorithms
description: Grade programming assignments using automated test suites, hold office hours twice weekly, moderate the course Piazza board, and assist in designing new coding projects for CS 1332.
qualifications: Completed Data Structures with an A. Proficiency in Java required; experience with JUnit testing frameworks preferred.

title: Teaching Assistant – General Biology I Lab
description: Supervise two weekly lab sections of 24 students, prepare reagents and specimens, grade lab reports, enforce safety protocols, and maintain inventory of lab supplies.
qualifications: Completion of General Biology I and II with a B+ or higher. Lab safety certification required within first two weeks.

title: Teaching Assistant – Macroeconomics
description: Lead review sessions before exams, grade problem sets and essays, hold weekly office hours, and update course materials on the LMS. Assist professor with lecture slide preparation.
qualifications: Graduate student in Economics. Strong quantitative background and experience with Stata or R preferred.

title: Teaching Assistant – American Government
institution: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
description: Facilitate discussion sections focused on the U.S. Constitution, federalism, and civil liberties. Grade written assignments and provide substantive feedback. Attend all lectures.
qualifications: Graduate enrollment in Political Science. Strong writing skills and ability to lead constructive classroom debate.

title: Teaching Assistant – Physics I (Mechanics)
description: Run two lab sections per week, assist students with experimental setups, grade lab reports and homework, and proctor exams for PHYS 2305. Attend weekly TA coordination meetings.
qualifications: Completed Physics II with a B+ or better. Familiarity with Vernier sensors and LoggerPro software is a plus.

title: Teaching Assistant – Spanish I
description: Lead two conversation practice sessions per week, grade written and oral assignments, hold office hours, and assist the instructor with cultural enrichment activities.
qualifications: Native or near-native Spanish proficiency. Graduate enrollment in Hispanic Studies, Linguistics, or related field.

title: Teaching Assistant – Technical Writing
description: Provide written feedback on student drafts, lead peer-review workshops, hold individual writing conferences, and assist in managing the course's Brightspace page for ENGL 42000.
qualifications: Graduate student in English, Rhetoric, or Professional Writing. Excellent grammar and editing skills required.

title: Teaching Assistant – Discrete Mathematics
description: Grade weekly proof-based assignments, hold recitation sections covering combinatorics, graph theory, and logic, and staff the course help desk. Develop supplemental study guides.
qualifications: Completed Discrete Math and at least one proof-based upper-level math course with an A. Strong ability to communicate abstract concepts.

title: Teaching Assistant – Music Theory I
description: Assist with ear-training lab sessions, grade theory homework and sight-singing assessments, hold weekly review sessions, and help students with notation software (Finale/Sibelius).
qualifications: Completion of Music Theory III with a B+ or higher. Proficiency in at least one notation software program.

title: Teaching Assistant – Introduction to Philosophy
description: Lead two weekly discussion sections on topics ranging from epistemology to ethics, grade argumentative essays, and provide detailed written feedback. Meet with professor to align on grading rubrics.
qualifications: Graduate enrollment in Philosophy. Strong background in formal logic and history of philosophy.

title: Teaching Assistant – Financial Accounting
description: Run review sessions covering journal entries, financial statements, and ratio analysis. Grade homework and case studies, manage Canvas gradebook, and hold four office hours per week.
qualifications: Completed Intermediate Accounting I and II with a B+ or better. CPA candidacy or MAcc enrollment preferred.

title: Teaching Assistant – Introduction to Computer Science (Python)
description: Staff the LaIR help room, lead a weekly section of 15 students, grade assignments using an autograder, attend TA prep meetings, and mentor first-time programmers in CS 106A.
qualifications: Completion of CS 106B with an A. Prior section-leading experience or completion of CS 198 preferred.

title: Teaching Assistant – Anatomy and Physiology I
description: Supervise cadaver lab sessions, assist students with organ identification, grade practical exams and written quizzes, and prepare lab materials. Enforce strict lab safety and dress code protocols.
qualifications: Completed A&P I and II with a B+ or higher. Must be comfortable working with human cadavers. Pre-med or nursing graduate students preferred.

title: Teaching Assistant – Statistics for the Social Sciences
description: Lead computer lab sessions using SPSS, grade homework and exams, hold office hours, and create supplemental instructional videos on topics like regression, ANOVA, and chi-square tests.
qualifications: Graduate student in Psychology, Sociology, or Education. Proficiency in SPSS required; R experience is a plus.

title: Teaching Assistant – Art History Survey
description: Lead discussion sections analyzing works from antiquity through the Renaissance, grade visual analysis essays, organize virtual museum tours, and assist with image database curation.
qualifications: Graduate student in Art History. Familiarity with ARTstor and museum databases preferred.

title: Teaching Assistant – Environmental Science
description: Lead field-based lab activities including water quality testing and soil sampling, grade lab reports, facilitate in-class group projects on sustainability, and manage course materials.
qualifications: Graduate enrollment in Environmental Studies or related field. Willingness to lead outdoor labs in variable weather conditions.

title: Undergraduate Research Assistant – Cognitive Neuroscience Lab
description: Recruit and screen research participants, administer EEG experiments, clean and preprocess neural data using MATLAB, maintain IRB documentation, and assist with literature reviews.
qualifications: Sophomore standing or above in Psychology or Neuroscience. Familiarity with MATLAB or Python preferred. Must complete CITI training.

title: Research Assistant – Machine Learning for Healthcare
description: Assist PI with developing deep learning models for medical image classification, preprocess large-scale clinical datasets, run experiments on GPU clusters, and co-author research papers.
qualifications: Graduate student in CS, Electrical Engineering, or Biomedical Informatics. Strong Python, PyTorch, and Linux command-line skills required.

title: Research Assistant – Behavioral Economics Lab
description: Design and program online experiments using oTree, recruit participants through Prolific, analyze survey data in R, and prepare results summaries for faculty review.
qualifications: Undergraduate junior or senior in Economics or Psychology. Experience with R or Stata and basic knowledge of experimental design.

title: Research Assistant – Structural Engineering Lab
description: Assist with fatigue testing of steel specimens, collect and process strain gauge data, maintain testing equipment, and help prepare technical reports and conference presentations.
qualifications: Graduate student in Civil or Structural Engineering. Experience with MTS testing machines and LabVIEW software preferred.

title: Research Assistant – Cancer Biology Lab
description: Perform cell culture, Western blots, and qPCR assays. Maintain lab notebooks, order supplies, manage tissue sample databases, and present findings at weekly lab meetings.
qualifications: Undergraduate senior or post-bac in Biology, Biochemistry, or related field. Hands-on experience with molecular biology techniques required.

title: Research Assistant – Natural Language Processing
description: Annotate text corpora for sentiment and entity recognition, fine-tune transformer models, evaluate model performance with standard NLP benchmarks, and contribute to open-source codebases.
qualifications: Graduate student in Computational Linguistics or CS. Proficiency in Python, HuggingFace Transformers, and familiarity with annotation tools like Prodigy.

title: Research Assistant – Climate Modeling
description: Run global climate simulations using CESM, process NetCDF output files, generate data visualizations in Python, and assist in drafting sections of journal manuscripts.
qualifications: Graduate student in Atmospheric Science, Earth Science, or Applied Math. Experience with Fortran, Python (xarray, matplotlib), and HPC environments.

title: Undergraduate Research Assistant – Political Polling Lab
description: Conduct phone and online surveys, enter and clean polling data, perform basic statistical analysis in SPSS, and help draft polling memos for publication.
qualifications: Undergraduate in Political Science or Statistics. Detail-oriented with strong data entry accuracy. Prior survey experience is a plus.

title: Research Assistant – Robotics and Autonomous Systems
description: Program autonomous drone navigation using ROS, collect and label LiDAR point cloud data, maintain hardware platforms, and participate in lab demonstrations and outreach events.
qualifications: Graduate student in Robotics, Mechanical Engineering, or CS. Proficiency in C++, ROS, and Linux. Experience with embedded systems is a plus.

title: Research Assistant – Developmental Psychology
description: Code video recordings of child-parent interactions, recruit families for longitudinal studies, administer standardized developmental assessments, and maintain participant databases.
qualifications: Undergraduate junior or senior in Psychology or Human Development. Must pass background check. Experience with Datavyu coding software preferred.

title: Research Assistant – Renewable Energy Materials
description: Synthesize perovskite thin films via spin coating, characterize samples using XRD and SEM, log experimental data, and assist in preparing manuscripts for peer-reviewed journals.
qualifications: Graduate student in Materials Science, Chemistry, or Physics. Cleanroom experience and familiarity with X-ray diffraction strongly preferred.

title: Research Assistant – Public Health Epidemiology
institution: Emory University Rollins School of Public Health
description: Clean and manage large epidemiological datasets, conduct statistical analyses using SAS, prepare tables and figures for CDC reports, and assist with systematic literature reviews.
qualifications: Graduate student in Epidemiology or Biostatistics. Proficiency in SAS or R required. Experience with REDCap is a plus.

title: Research Assistant – Marine Biology Field Station
description: Conduct reef surveys using SCUBA, identify coral species, maintain aquarium systems, collect water quality samples, and enter field data into relational databases.
qualifications: Undergraduate or graduate student in Marine Biology. AAUS Scientific Diver certification required. Must be comfortable working in open-ocean conditions.

title: Research Assistant – Quantum Computing Theory
description: Derive and simulate quantum error correction codes, implement algorithms in Qiskit, analyze simulation results, and prepare technical writeups for group seminars.
qualifications: Graduate student in Physics, CS, or Mathematics. Strong linear algebra background and experience with quantum computing frameworks required.

title: Research Assistant – Sociology of Education
description: Transcribe and code qualitative interviews, manage NVivo codebooks, conduct literature searches, and draft sections of grant proposals for NSF-funded projects.
qualifications: Graduate student in Sociology or Education Policy. Qualitative research methods experience required. Bilingual English/Spanish is a strong plus.

title: Research Assistant – Biomedical Imaging Lab
description: Process MRI and CT scan data, develop image segmentation algorithms in Python, maintain imaging databases, and present findings at departmental research symposia.
qualifications: Graduate student in Biomedical Engineering or CS. Experience with ITK/VTK, SimpleITK, or 3D Slicer preferred.

title: Undergraduate Research Assistant – Archaeology Field Project
description: Participate in excavation of Hohokam sites, catalog and photograph artifacts, assist with GIS mapping of dig sites, and help maintain the department's artifact repository.
qualifications: Undergraduate in Anthropology or Archaeology. Field school completion preferred. Must be able to work outdoors in high heat.

title: Research Assistant – Computational Genomics
description: Build bioinformatics pipelines for whole-genome sequencing data, perform variant calling using GATK, write reproducible analysis workflows in Nextflow, and visualize results.
qualifications: Graduate student in Bioinformatics, Computational Biology, or CS. Proficiency in Python, R, and bash scripting. Familiarity with cloud computing (Terra/GCP) preferred.

title: Research Assistant – Social Media and Democracy
description: Collect and analyze social media datasets related to election integrity, code content for misinformation indicators, draft research briefs, and maintain data collection scripts.
qualifications: Graduate student in Communication, Political Science, or CS. Experience with Python (Pandas, network analysis libraries) and social media APIs.

title: Research Assistant – Exercise Physiology Lab
description: Conduct VO2 max tests and body composition assessments, calibrate metabolic carts, manage participant scheduling, and analyze exercise data using specialized software.
qualifications: Undergraduate senior or graduate student in Kinesiology or Exercise Science. CPR/AED certification required.

title: Student Nurse Extern – Medical-Surgical Unit
description: Work alongside RN preceptors providing direct patient care on a 36-bed med-surg unit. Perform assessments, administer medications under supervision, document in Epic EHR, and participate in interdisciplinary rounds.
qualifications: Currently enrolled in an accredited BSN program with completion of at least one clinical rotation. BLS certification required.

title: Student Nurse Extern – Pediatric Emergency Department
description: Assist with triage, vital signs, specimen collection, and wound care for pediatric patients. Shadow RNs during critical interventions and maintain patient comfort during procedures.
qualifications: BSN student who has completed Pediatric Nursing clinical. PALS certification preferred. Must pass background check and drug screen.

title: Student Nurse Extern – Labor and Delivery
description: Support L&D nurses with fetal monitoring, patient education, newborn assessments, and postpartum care. Assist in operating room during scheduled C-sections under RN supervision.
qualifications: BSN student with completion of OB/Maternal Health clinical rotation. NRP certification preferred. Must be comfortable in fast-paced environment.

title: Student Nurse Extern – Oncology Unit
description: Provide compassionate care to adult oncology patients including symptom management, medication administration, central line dressing changes, and emotional support. Document in Epic.
qualifications: BSN student with at least two completed clinical rotations. Oncology Nursing Society student membership preferred. Strong empathy and communication skills.

title: Student Nurse Extern – Intensive Care Unit
description: Shadow ICU nurses caring for critically ill patients, assist with hemodynamic monitoring, ventilator checks, medication titration documentation, and family communication.
qualifications: BSN student in final year of program. Completion of Critical Care Nursing course required. ACLS certification preferred.

title: Clinical Research Coordinator Assistant
description: Screen patients for clinical trial eligibility, obtain informed consent, schedule study visits, maintain regulatory binders, enter data into REDCap, and coordinate with sponsor monitors.
qualifications: Undergraduate or graduate student in Nursing, Public Health, or Biology. CITI training completion required. Detail-oriented with strong organizational skills.

title: Nursing Simulation Lab Assistant
institution: University of Virginia School of Nursing
description: Set up high-fidelity simulation mannequins, prepare clinical scenarios, operate AV equipment during debriefings, maintain supply inventory, and reset stations between student groups.
qualifications: Nursing student who has completed Fundamentals of Nursing. Familiarity with SimMan or Laerdal mannequins preferred.

title: Community Health Outreach Worker (Student Position)
institution: University of New Mexico Health Sciences Center
description: Conduct health screenings at community events, distribute educational materials on diabetes prevention, assist with vaccination clinics, and collect survey data for public health research.
qualifications: Enrolled in Nursing, Public Health, or Social Work program. Bilingual English/Spanish strongly preferred. Valid driver's license required.

title: Student Physical Therapy Aide
description: Assist licensed physical therapists with patient exercises, set up treatment areas, clean and maintain equipment, schedule patient appointments, and document treatment notes.
qualifications: Undergraduate student in Exercise Science, Kinesiology, or Pre-PT track. CPR certification required. Must be able to lift 50 lbs.

title: Student Dental Hygienist Assistant
institution: University of Michigan School of Dentistry
description: Assist dental hygiene students and faculty with patient setups, sterilize instruments, take and develop dental radiographs under supervision, and manage patient appointment schedules.
qualifications: Enrolled in Dental Hygiene or Pre-Dental program. Radiology certification preferred. Must follow strict infection control protocols.

title: Pharmacy Technician Student Intern
institution: Ohio State University Wexner Medical Center
description: Fill and verify prescription orders, compound medications under pharmacist supervision, manage inventory using automated dispensing cabinets, and counsel patients on medication usage.
qualifications: Enrolled in an accredited Pharm.D. program. State pharmacy intern license required. Familiarity with Epic Willow preferred.

title: Student Occupational Therapy Assistant
description: Support OT sessions for pediatric clients, prepare adaptive equipment and sensory materials, document session notes, and assist with group therapy activities.
qualifications: Enrolled in OT or OTA program. Experience working with children with developmental disabilities preferred. Must pass CORI check.

title: Student Radiologic Technologist
institution: University of Iowa Hospitals and Clinics
description: Perform diagnostic X-rays under technologist supervision, position patients, apply radiation safety protocols, maintain imaging equipment, and enter patient data into PACS systems.
qualifications: Enrolled in JRCERT-accredited Radiologic Technology program. Must have completed clinical prerequisites. ARRT student membership required.

title: Medical Scribe – Emergency Department
institution: University of Florida Health Shands Hospital
description: Accompany EM physicians during patient encounters, document histories, physical exams, and medical decision-making in real time using Epic. Assist with chart completion and order entry.
qualifications: Pre-med undergraduate with strong medical terminology knowledge. Typing speed of 60+ WPM. Must complete ScribeAmerica or equivalent training program.

title: Student Dietetic Technician
description: Conduct nutritional screenings, assist registered dietitians with meal planning for patients with chronic conditions, track caloric intake data, and provide nutrition education materials.
qualifications: Enrolled in a Dietetics or Nutrition Science program. ServSafe certification preferred.

title: Library Circulation Desk Assistant
description: Check in/out materials, shelve returned items, assist patrons with catalog searches, process interlibrary loan requests, and open/close the library during assigned shifts.
qualifications: Currently enrolled undergraduate. Must be able to work evenings and weekends. Attention to detail and customer service orientation required.

title: Digital Archives Student Assistant
description: Digitize rare manuscripts and photographs using flatbed and overhead scanners, create metadata records in Dublin Core, perform quality control checks, and organize digital files.
qualifications: Undergraduate or graduate student in Library Science, History, or Digital Humanities. Experience with metadata standards and Adobe Photoshop preferred.

title: Writing Center Tutor
description: Conduct one-on-one writing consultations with undergraduate and graduate students across all disciplines. Help with brainstorming, thesis development, organization, and citation formatting.
qualifications: Undergraduate junior or above with a 3.5+ GPA. Strong writing skills across genres. Must complete 20-hour tutor training program.

title: Math Tutoring Center Peer Tutor
description: Provide drop-in tutoring for students in College Algebra through Differential Equations. Explain concepts, work through practice problems, and track student visit data in TutorTrac.
qualifications: Completed Calculus III with a B+ or higher. Patient and effective at explaining complex mathematical concepts to diverse learners.

title: Academic Success Coach (Peer Mentor)
description: Meet weekly with 10–12 first-year students to set academic goals, develop study strategies, connect them with campus resources, and track their progress through the semester.
qualifications: Sophomore standing or above with a 3.3+ GPA. Completed peer mentor training. Strong interpersonal skills and empathy.

title: Disability Services Note-Taker
description: Attend lectures and take comprehensive, organized notes for students registered with the Office of Disability Services. Upload notes to the ODS portal within 24 hours of each class.
qualifications: Undergraduate enrolled in the relevant course with a B+ or better. Excellent note-taking and organizational skills.

title: Language Lab Monitor
description: Supervise the language learning lab, assist students with audio/video equipment, troubleshoot software issues, manage lab scheduling, and maintain equipment inventory.
qualifications: Undergraduate in Linguistics, Foreign Languages, or related field. Tech-savvy with basic troubleshooting skills. Multilingual ability is a plus.

title: STEM Learning Center Lab Assistant
description: Staff the STEM center computer lab, assist students with MATLAB, Python, and Excel assignments, maintain printer supplies, and track daily usage statistics.
qualifications: Undergraduate in a STEM field. Proficiency in at least two programming languages. Friendly and patient demeanor.

title: Test Proctoring Assistant
description: Administer exams in the university testing center, verify student IDs, enforce academic integrity policies, troubleshoot computer-based testing software, and maintain exam security.
qualifications: Undergraduate with clean academic integrity record. Must be punctual and able to maintain confidentiality.

title: Supplemental Instruction Leader – Chemistry
description: Attend all General Chemistry lectures, plan and facilitate three weekly SI sessions using collaborative learning strategies, create study materials, and report attendance data to the SI coordinator.
qualifications: Completed General Chemistry I and II with an A. Must attend SI Leader training workshop. Strong facilitation skills.

title: Resident Advisor (RA)
description: Serve as a live-in peer mentor for 40–50 residents. Plan monthly programming, mediate roommate conflicts, enforce housing policies, respond to emergencies, and serve on-call rotations.
qualifications: Undergraduate sophomore or above with a 2.5+ GPA. Must live on campus. Leadership experience and conflict resolution skills required. Includes room and board.

title: Campus Recreation Lifeguard
description: Monitor pool areas, enforce safety rules, perform water rescues when necessary, maintain chemical logs, and conduct facility checks. May also teach swim lessons.
qualifications: Current American Red Cross Lifeguard certification. CPR/AED and First Aid certifications required. WSI certification preferred.

title: Student Event Coordinator
description: Plan and execute campus events including concerts, cultural celebrations, and speaker series. Manage budgets, coordinate with vendors, promote events on social media, and handle day-of logistics.
qualifications: Undergraduate with event planning or student org leadership experience. Strong project management and communication skills.

title: Campus Tour Guide
description: Lead walking tours for prospective students and families, present university history and programs enthusiastically, answer questions about campus life, and participate in Admitted Students Day events.
qualifications: Undergraduate with a 3.0+ GPA and strong public speaking skills. Must complete tour guide training. Genuine passion for the university.

title: Intramural Sports Referee
description: Officiate intramural basketball, flag football, soccer, and volleyball games. Enforce rules, manage game clocks, resolve disputes, and submit game reports after each contest.
qualifications: Knowledge of sport rules for at least two intramural sports. Must attend referee training clinics. Calm under pressure with fair judgment.

title: Student Government Office Assistant
description: Provide administrative support to the Student Council, manage meeting schedules and minutes, maintain the SGA website, process student organization funding requests, and respond to student inquiries.
qualifications: Undergraduate with strong organizational skills. Proficiency in Google Workspace. Interest in student governance preferred.

title: Sustainability Office Student Intern
description: Assist with campus waste audits, promote recycling and composting programs, collect utility data for sustainability reports, and plan Earth Week events. Manage social media content.
qualifications: Undergraduate in Environmental Studies, Sustainability, or related field. Passion for environmental advocacy. Social media management experience preferred.

title: Campus Dining Student Supervisor
description: Supervise a team of 8–10 student dining workers per shift, ensure food safety compliance, manage opening/closing procedures, handle customer complaints, and track inventory levels.
qualifications: Undergraduate with prior food service experience. ServSafe certification required within 30 days of hire. Leadership and time management skills essential.

title: Fitness Center Front Desk Attendant
description: Check student IDs at entry, answer facility questions, maintain towel service, issue equipment, report maintenance needs, and ensure gym safety policies are followed.
qualifications: Currently enrolled undergraduate. CPR certification preferred. Friendly and helpful attitude. Ability to work early morning or late evening shifts.

title: Student Parking Services Ambassador
description: Monitor campus parking lots, issue citations for violations, assist visitors with directions, operate parking gates during events, and process daily permit verification reports.
qualifications: Enrolled undergraduate with a valid driver's license. Must be comfortable working outdoors. Strong conflict-resolution skills for handling parking disputes.

title: Help Desk Technician (Student Position)
description: Provide Tier 1 technical support via phone, email, and walk-in. Troubleshoot Wi-Fi connectivity, email configurations, VPN issues, and software installations. Log all tickets in ServiceNow.
qualifications: Undergraduate in CS, IT, or related field. Familiarity with Windows, macOS, and basic networking concepts. Customer service skills essential.

title: Web Developer – Student Affairs Office
description: Build and maintain internal web applications using React and Node.js, update the Student Affairs website, implement accessibility (WCAG 2.1) improvements, and manage WordPress content.
qualifications: Undergraduate or graduate student with proficiency in HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and React. Experience with REST APIs and Git version control required.

title: Database Administrator Student Assistant
description: Assist senior DBAs with monitoring database performance, writing SQL queries for data extraction, maintaining backup schedules, and documenting database schemas and procedures.
qualifications: Undergraduate junior or above in CS or Information Systems. Proficiency in SQL (PostgreSQL or MySQL). Basic knowledge of database administration concepts.

title: Cybersecurity Intern
description: Assist the security operations team with vulnerability scanning, log analysis using Splunk, phishing simulation campaigns, and incident response documentation. Participate in tabletop exercises.
qualifications: Junior or senior in Cybersecurity, CS, or IT. CompTIA Security+ or equivalent knowledge. Familiarity with NIST frameworks and common security tools.

title: Mobile App Developer Intern
description: Develop features for the university's official campus app using React Native, integrate with campus APIs for dining, events, and transit data, and conduct user testing sessions.
qualifications: Undergraduate or graduate student in CS or Software Engineering. Experience with React Native or Flutter. Familiarity with REST APIs and mobile UI/UX principles.

title: Data Analyst Student Worker – Institutional Research
description: Clean and analyze enrollment, retention, and graduation data. Build Tableau dashboards for university leadership, write SQL queries against the data warehouse, and prepare reports for accreditation.
qualifications: Graduate student in Statistics, Data Science, or Higher Education. Proficiency in SQL, Tableau, and R or Python. Experience with FERPA-regulated data preferred.

title: Classroom Technology Support Specialist
description: Set up AV equipment for lectures and events, troubleshoot projectors, microphones, and Zoom conferencing systems, train faculty on classroom technology, and maintain equipment inventory.
qualifications: Undergraduate with AV/tech experience. Familiarity with Crestron, Extron, or similar AV control systems. Excellent troubleshooting skills under time pressure.

title: UX Research Assistant
institution: Carnegie Mellon University Human-Computer Interaction Institute
description: Recruit participants for usability studies, conduct moderated usability tests, analyze qualitative data using affinity diagramming, and present findings to faculty researchers.
qualifications: Graduate student in HCI, Design, or Psychology. Experience with Figma, UserTesting.com, or similar platforms. Strong qualitative analysis skills.

title: IT Project Management Intern
description: Support the IT PMO by tracking project milestones in Jira, facilitating standup meetings, maintaining Confluence documentation, and preparing status reports for stakeholders.
qualifications: Undergraduate junior or senior in IT, Business, or related field. Familiarity with Agile/Scrum methodology. Experience with Jira and Confluence preferred.

title: Machine Learning Engineering Intern
description: Build and deploy ML models for campus energy optimization, preprocess IoT sensor data, train models using scikit-learn and TensorFlow, and create monitoring dashboards.
qualifications: Graduate student in CS, Data Science, or Electrical Engineering. Strong Python skills with experience in ML frameworks. Familiarity with Docker and cloud platforms.

title: Cloud Infrastructure Student Engineer
description: Assist in managing AWS cloud infrastructure, write Infrastructure-as-Code using Terraform, monitor system health with CloudWatch, and automate routine tasks with Python scripts.
qualifications: Junior or senior in CS or IT. AWS Cloud Practitioner certification preferred. Experience with Linux, Python, and IaC tools.

title: GIS Technician Student Assistant
description: Create and update campus GIS layers, digitize maps, perform spatial analysis for facilities planning, maintain geodatabases, and generate maps for university publications.
qualifications: Undergraduate or graduate student in Geography or GIS. Proficiency in ArcGIS Pro required. Experience with Python scripting for GIS is a plus.

title: Social Media Coordinator (Student Position)
description: Create and schedule content for the university's Instagram, TikTok, and X accounts. Photograph campus events, track engagement metrics, and collaborate with the communications team on campaigns.
qualifications: Undergraduate with a strong personal portfolio of social media content. Proficiency in Canva and basic video editing. Photography skills a plus.

title: Student Journalist – Campus Newspaper
institution: University of Missouri School of Journalism
description: Report on campus news, student government, and local events for the Columbia Missourian. Conduct interviews, write articles on deadline, fact-check sources, and attend editorial meetings.
qualifications: Undergraduate in Journalism or Communications. Strong AP Style writing. Previous byline experience preferred but not required.

title: Graphic Design Student Assistant
description: Design flyers, posters, social media graphics, and email templates for campus departments. Maintain brand consistency, manage design request queue, and archive completed assets.
qualifications: Undergraduate in Graphic Design or Visual Communication. Proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite (Illustrator, InDesign, Photoshop). Strong typography skills.

title: Video Production Assistant
description: Assist with filming campus events, student profiles, and promotional videos. Operate cameras, manage lighting and audio setups, edit footage in Premiere Pro, and maintain equipment inventory.
qualifications: Undergraduate in Film, Media Production, or Communications. Experience with Adobe Premiere Pro and DSLR cameras. Portfolio or reel required.

title: Alumni Relations Student Caller
description: Contact alumni via phone to share university updates, solicit annual fund donations, update contact information, and log call outcomes in the CRM system.
qualifications: Undergraduate with strong interpersonal and persuasion skills. Comfortable making cold calls. Evening availability required.

title: Podcast Producer – University Radio Station
description: Research and book guests, write show outlines, record and edit episodes in Audacity/GarageBand, manage RSS feeds, and promote episodes on social media channels.
qualifications: Undergraduate in Journalism, Media, or Communications. Audio editing experience required. Knowledge of podcast hosting platforms (Anchor, Buzzsprout).

title: Content Writer – University Blog
description: Write 2–3 blog posts per week on student life, campus events, and academic programs. Conduct interviews with students and faculty, optimize content for SEO, and manage the WordPress editorial calendar.
qualifications: Undergraduate in English, Journalism, or Marketing. Strong narrative writing skills. SEO knowledge and WordPress experience preferred.

title: Photography Assistant – Athletics Department
description: Photograph home athletic events including football, basketball, and track. Edit photos in Lightroom, upload to the media asset management system, and assist with game-day promotional materials.
qualifications: Undergraduate with a photography portfolio. Own DSLR camera with telephoto lens preferred. Experience shooting fast-action sports. Lightroom proficiency required.

title: Public Relations Intern – University Communications
description: Draft press releases, compile media monitoring reports, pitch stories to local media, assist with crisis communication planning, and manage the department's media contact database.
qualifications: Junior or senior in PR, Communications, or Journalism. Excellent writing skills. Familiarity with Cision or Meltwater preferred.

title: Email Marketing Student Assistant
description: Design and send weekly email newsletters using Mailchimp, segment mailing lists, A/B test subject lines, analyze open and click-through rates, and maintain email compliance with CAN-SPAM.
qualifications: Undergraduate in Marketing or Communications. Experience with Mailchimp or similar ESP. Basic HTML/CSS knowledge for email template customization.

title: Department Office Student Worker – Biology
description: Answer phones, greet visitors, sort mail, process purchase orders, file documents, update faculty directories, and assist with event coordination for departmental seminars.
qualifications: Currently enrolled undergraduate. Proficiency in Microsoft Office. Professional demeanor and strong organizational skills.

title: Financial Aid Office Student Assistant
description: Assist students with FAFSA questions at the front desk, process document verification requests, scan and index financial aid documents, and answer phone inquiries about aid packages.
qualifications: Undergraduate with knowledge of financial aid processes. Bilingual English/Spanish preferred. FERPA training required within first week.

title: Admissions Office Student Ambassador
institution: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
description: Conduct campus tours, assist with admissions events and open houses, review and sort application materials, answer prospective student emails, and represent the university at college fairs.
qualifications: Sophomore or above with a 3.2+ GPA. Exceptional communication skills. Genuine enthusiasm for sharing the university experience.

title: Student Payroll Processing Assistant
description: Process bi-weekly student payroll, verify timesheets, resolve payroll discrepancies, update employee records in PeopleSoft, and respond to payroll-related inquiries from student workers.
qualifications: Junior or senior in Accounting, Finance, or HR. Experience with PeopleSoft or similar HRIS. High attention to detail and ability to handle confidential information.

title: International Student Services Assistant
description: Help international students navigate visa processes, organize cultural orientation events, maintain SEVIS records, assist with OPT/CPT applications, and staff the international student resource desk.
qualifications: Undergraduate or graduate student. Experience navigating U.S. immigration processes preferred. Multilingual ability strongly valued. Sensitivity to cross-cultural communication.

title: Career Services Peer Advisor
description: Conduct resume and cover letter reviews, facilitate mock interview sessions, help students navigate Handshake job postings, and present career workshops on topics like LinkedIn optimization.
qualifications: Junior or senior with prior internship experience. Strong knowledge of resume best practices. Completion of peer advisor training program required.

title: Study Abroad Office Student Worker
description: Assist students with study abroad applications, process credit transfer paperwork, organize pre-departure orientations, maintain the study abroad database, and create promotional materials.
qualifications: Undergraduate who has completed a study abroad program. Organized and detail-oriented. Experience with databases and Microsoft Office.

title: Graduate School Application Reviewer (Student Worker)
description: Assist admissions staff with preliminary review of graduate applications, verify completeness of transcripts and recommendation letters, update applicant status in Slate CRM, and compile statistics.
qualifications: Graduate student in any discipline. Strong attention to detail. Familiarity with Slate or similar CRM. Must handle confidential records responsibly.

title: Student Legal Services Intake Assistant
description: Schedule consultations for the student legal services office, conduct intake interviews, organize case files, research basic legal questions under attorney supervision, and maintain client confidentiality.
qualifications: Pre-law undergraduate or paralegal studies student. Strong organizational and interpersonal skills. Must sign confidentiality agreement.

title: Registrar's Office Data Entry Clerk (Student)
description: Enter and verify student records in the SIS, process transcript requests, update course catalog information, assist with enrollment certification letters, and handle front-desk inquiries.
qualifications: Undergraduate with excellent data entry accuracy (95%+ required). Familiarity with student information systems is a plus. FERPA compliance training required.

title: Lab Technician – Chemical Engineering Processes Lab
description: Prepare chemical reagents and solutions for undergraduate lab courses, calibrate lab instruments, maintain MSDS binders, assist students during distillation and reactor experiments, and clean/restock stations.
qualifications: Undergraduate junior or senior in Chemical Engineering. Completed Unit Operations lab. Knowledge of OSHA lab safety standards.

title: Makerspace Student Technician
description: Supervise the student makerspace during open hours, train users on 3D printers, laser cutters, and CNC mills, perform equipment maintenance, and enforce safety rules.
qualifications: Undergraduate in any engineering discipline. Hands-on experience with rapid prototyping tools. Must complete makerspace safety certification.

title: Electronics Shop Student Assistant
description: Assist faculty and graduate students with PCB soldering, circuit debugging, and oscilloscope measurements. Maintain inventory of electronic components and organize the electronics shop workspace.
qualifications: Undergraduate in EE or CE. Experience with soldering, multimeters, and basic circuit analysis. Familiarity with KiCad or Eagle PCB design software preferred.

title: Civil Engineering Survey Crew Member (Student)
description: Operate total stations and GPS receivers for campus construction surveys, process field data using Civil 3D, maintain survey equipment, and prepare boundary and topographic maps.
qualifications: Undergraduate in Civil Engineering. Completed Surveying course. Ability to work outdoors in Texas heat. Valid driver's license.

title: Aerospace Wind Tunnel Lab Assistant
description: Set up and operate subsonic wind tunnel experiments, calibrate pressure transducers and force balances, assist students with data acquisition, and maintain test models and instrumentation.
qualifications: Undergraduate in Aerospace or Mechanical Engineering. Completed Aerodynamics with a B+ or higher. Experience with LabVIEW preferred.

title: Bioengineering Tissue Culture Lab Assistant
description: Maintain mammalian cell cultures, prepare growth media, perform cell counts using a hemocytometer, autoclave lab equipment, and keep detailed lab notebook entries following GLP guidelines.
qualifications: Undergraduate in Bioengineering, Biology, or related field. Sterile technique training required. Experience with biosafety cabinets preferred.

title: Theater Stage Crew Student Worker
institution: Carnegie Mellon University School of Drama
description: Build and paint set pieces, operate fly systems and lighting boards during performances, assist with load-in and strike, and maintain backstage organization during the production season.
qualifications: Undergraduate in any major. Prior stage crew or technical theater experience. Ability to lift 50+ lbs and work on ladders. Evening and weekend availability required.

title: Museum Gallery Attendant (Student Position)
description: Monitor gallery spaces, engage visitors with information about current exhibitions, ensure artwork safety, assist with special events, and help with gallery installations and deinstallations.
qualifications: Undergraduate in Art History, Museum Studies, or related field. Strong public speaking and customer service skills. Interest in visual arts.

title: Costume Shop Student Stitcher
institution: University of North Carolina School of the Arts
description: Sew costumes for departmental theater and dance productions, alter garments for fittings, organize fabric storage, maintain sewing machines, and assist the costume designer with fabric sourcing.
qualifications: Undergraduate with intermediate sewing skills. Experience with both hand and machine sewing. Knowledge of pattern reading preferred.

title: Music Practice Room Monitor
description: Manage practice room reservations, enforce booking policies, issue and collect instrument locker keys, monitor noise levels, and report maintenance issues for pianos and practice rooms.
qualifications: Currently enrolled music student. Responsible and punctual. Evening and weekend availability required.

title: Creative Writing Workshop Facilitator
description: Lead weekly undergraduate creative writing workshops, provide constructive feedback on fiction and poetry submissions, facilitate peer critique sessions, and organize open mic events.
qualifications: MFA student in Creative Writing. Publication credits preferred. Experience facilitating writing groups and giving developmental feedback.

title: Dance Studio Assistant
institution: New York University Tisch School of the Arts
description: Open and close dance studios, manage booking schedules, set up sound systems for classes and rehearsals, maintain sprung floor surfaces, and ensure studio cleanliness between sessions.
qualifications: Undergraduate in Dance or any major. Familiarity with professional audio equipment. Reliable and available for early morning/late evening shifts.

title: Art Studio Lab Monitor
institution: Savannah College of Art and Design
description: Supervise open studio hours for ceramics, printmaking, and sculpture labs. Monitor kiln firings, maintain printmaking presses, enforce safety procedures, and manage supply checkouts.
qualifications: Undergraduate in Fine Arts. Experience with at least one studio medium (ceramics, printmaking, or sculpture). Must complete kiln safety training.

title: Film Editing Suite Student Monitor
institution: University of Southern California School of Cinematic Arts
description: Manage editing suite reservations, troubleshoot Avid and DaVinci Resolve workstations, assist students with file management and codec issues, and maintain hardware including monitors and audio interfaces.
qualifications: Undergraduate in Film Production. Proficiency in Avid Media Composer and DaVinci Resolve. Knowledge of professional video codecs and file formats.

title: Student Venture Capital Analyst
description: Research early-stage startups for the university's student-run venture fund, build financial models, prepare investment memos, conduct due diligence calls, and present recommendations to the investment committee.
qualifications: MBA or undergraduate senior in Finance or Entrepreneurship. Experience with financial modeling in Excel. Strong analytical and presentation skills.

title: Student Accounting Clerk – Bursar's Office
description: Process tuition payments, reconcile daily cash receipts, assist students with billing inquiries, update account records in PeopleSoft, and help prepare monthly financial reports.
qualifications: Undergraduate in Accounting or Finance. Familiarity with PeopleSoft or similar ERP. Strong attention to detail and ability to handle sensitive financial information.

title: Entrepreneurship Center Student Coordinator
description: Manage event logistics for pitch competitions and startup workshops, maintain the center's CRM of student entrepreneurs, schedule mentor meetings, and track program participation metrics.
qualifications: Undergraduate in Business or Entrepreneurship. Event coordination experience. Strong organizational and communication skills.

title: Investment Club Research Analyst (Paid Position)
institution: University of Virginia McIntire School of Commerce
description: Conduct equity research on assigned sectors, build DCF and comparable company models, write investment theses, present stock pitches to the investment committee, and monitor portfolio positions.
qualifications: Undergraduate junior or senior in Commerce or Economics. Bloomberg Terminal experience preferred. CFA Level I candidacy is a plus.

title: Supply Chain Management Intern
institution: Michigan State University Broad College of Business
description: Analyze procurement data, assist with vendor performance evaluations, map supply chain processes, build dashboards in Power BI, and support the department's lean management initiatives.
qualifications: Undergraduate in Supply Chain Management or Operations. Proficiency in Excel and Power BI. APICS certification coursework is a plus.

title: Student Peer Counselor – Counseling Center
description: Provide initial intake screening for students seeking counseling services, facilitate psychoeducational workshops on stress management and mindfulness, and refer students to appropriate campus resources.
qualifications: Undergraduate senior or graduate student in Psychology or Counseling. Completion of peer counselor training program. Empathetic and nonjudgmental communication style.

title: Crisis Text Line Student Volunteer Coordinator
description: Recruit and train student volunteers for the campus crisis support line, schedule shifts, debrief volunteers after difficult calls, and maintain training documentation.
qualifications: Graduate student in Social Work or Counseling Psychology. Completion of crisis intervention training. Previous hotline volunteering experience required.

title: Student Case Manager – Dean of Students Office
description: Assist the Dean of Students team with student follow-ups, connect students to food pantry, housing, and financial emergency resources, maintain case notes, and help coordinate wellness check protocols.
qualifications: Graduate student in Social Work, Higher Education, or Public Health. Strong interpersonal skills and knowledge of campus support systems. FERPA training required.

title: Substance Abuse Prevention Educator (Student Position)
description: Deliver AlcoholEdu presentations to first-year residence halls, facilitate bystander intervention workshops, create social media campaigns around responsible decision-making, and collect program evaluation data.
qualifications: Undergraduate with training in health education or public health. Comfortable presenting to large groups. Must complete motivational interviewing training.

title: Sexual Assault Prevention Advocate (Student Worker)
description: Provide confidential support to survivors, accompany students to Title IX appointments, facilitate consent education workshops, staff the advocacy office, and maintain resource materials.
qualifications: Undergraduate or graduate student. Completion of 40-hour advocate training. Trauma-informed communication skills. Must maintain strict confidentiality.

title: Campus Farm Student Worker

qualifications: Undergraduate in Agriculture, Plant Sciences, or Environmental Studies. Willingness to perform physical labor outdoors. Organic farming interest preferred.

title: Forestry Field Technician (Student Summer Position)
institution: Oregon State University College of Forestry
description: Conduct forest inventory plots, measure tree diameter and height, collect soil samples, identify native and invasive plant species, and enter data into the college's research database.
qualifications: Undergraduate in Forestry, Natural Resources, or Biology. Physical fitness for field work. Dendrology coursework preferred. Valid driver's license required.

title: Animal Science Research Farm Hand

qualifications: Undergraduate in Animal Science or Veterinary Technology. Experience handling large animals. Willingness to work early mornings, weekends, and in all weather conditions.

title: Greenhouse Student Assistant
description: Maintain greenhouse plants for teaching and research, monitor temperature and humidity systems, water and fertilize according to schedules, propagate plants, and assist faculty with experimental setups.
qualifications: Undergraduate in Plant Science, Horticulture, or Biology. Basic plant care knowledge. Responsible and able to work independently.

title: Water Quality Lab Student Technician

qualifications: Undergraduate in Environmental Engineering, Chemistry, or Ecology. Lab safety training. Valid driver's license for field trips. Familiarity with spectrophotometry a plus.

title: Legal Research Assistant – Constitutional Law Clinic

qualifications: Law student (1L or above). Strong legal research and writing skills. Familiarity with Bluebook citation format. Interest in constitutional law.

title: Policy Research Intern – Education Policy Center
description: Research K-12 education policy issues, analyze federal and state legislation, compile data for policy briefs, attend congressional hearings, and draft blog posts for the center's website.
qualifications: Graduate student in Public Policy, Education, or Political Science. Strong quantitative and writing skills. Interest in education reform.

title: Student Compliance Assistant – Title IX Office
description: Support the Title IX coordinator with case file organization, schedule investigation interviews, draft correspondence, maintain the case management database, and assist with policy training materials.
qualifications: Junior or senior in Pre-Law, Social Work, or Public Policy. Strong discretion with confidential information. Excellent writing and organizational skills.

title: Immigration Law Clinic Student Worker
institution: University of California, Irvine School of Law

qualifications: Law student (2L or above). Fluency in Spanish required. Immigration law coursework completed or in progress. Sensitivity to vulnerable populations.

title: Research Assistant – Applied Linguistics
description: Transcribe and code bilingual speech samples, analyze data using Praat and R, recruit participants from multilingual communities, and assist with grant writing for NSF proposals.
qualifications: Graduate student in Linguistics or Applied Linguistics. Experience with phonetic analysis software. Bilingual proficiency preferred.

title: Research Assistant – Food Science and Safety

qualifications: Graduate student in Food Science or Microbiology. Aseptic technique and BSL-2 lab experience required. Familiarity with HACCP principles.

title: Research Assistant – Sports Psychology Lab

qualifications: Undergraduate senior or graduate student in Psychology or Kinesiology. CITI training required. Experience with SPSS or R. Interest in sport and performance psychology.

title: Research Assistant – Urban Planning and Housing
description: Collect and geocode housing data, conduct spatial analysis in ArcGIS, create data visualizations for policy presentations, interview community stakeholders, and assist with grant reports.
qualifications: Graduate student in Urban Planning, Public Policy, or Geography. GIS proficiency required. Mixed-methods research experience preferred.

title: Research Assistant – Astrophysics Observation Lab
description: Reduce astronomical imaging data using IRAF and Python, assist with telescope operations on Mount Lemmon, maintain observation logs, and contribute to exoplanet detection pipelines.
qualifications: Graduate student in Astronomy or Physics. Experience with Python (astropy, numpy). Willingness to work overnight observation shifts.

title: Teaching Assistant – World History to 1500
description: Lead two weekly discussion sections, grade document-based essays and map quizzes, hold office hours, and collaborate with the professor on primary source selection.
qualifications: Graduate student in History. Broad knowledge of pre-modern world history. Strong facilitation skills for classroom discussions.

title: Teaching Assistant – Introduction to Engineering Design
description: Supervise freshman design teams building prototype projects, teach SolidWorks CAD sessions, grade design reports and presentations, and maintain lab equipment and 3D printers.
qualifications: Graduate student in any Engineering discipline. Proficiency in SolidWorks or similar CAD software. Experience mentoring teams preferred.

title: Teaching Assistant – Microeconomics

qualifications: Graduate student in Economics. Completed Intermediate Micro with distinction. Experience with mathematical economics and clear verbal explanation of models.

title: Teaching Assistant – Introduction to Linguistics
description: Lead discussion sections covering phonetics, morphology, syntax, and semantics. Grade homework sets and exams, hold weekly office hours, and assist with preparing lecture materials.
qualifications: Graduate student in Linguistics. Completed core Linguistics coursework. Experience with IPA transcription. Clear and patient teaching style.

title: Teaching Assistant – Fundamentals of Nursing

qualifications: MSN student or BSN graduate with clinical experience. Current RN license. BLS instructor certification preferred.

title: Teaching Assistant – Linear Algebra
description: Run weekly recitations on vector spaces, eigenvalues, and matrix decompositions. Grade proofs and computational assignments, hold office hours, and create review materials for exams.
qualifications: Graduate student in Mathematics. Completed graduate-level algebra. Ability to explain abstract concepts with concrete examples.

title: Backend Engineering Intern
description: Design and implement RESTful APIs using Node.js and Express, write database migrations in PostgreSQL, set up CI/CD pipelines with GitHub Actions, and write unit and integration tests.
qualifications: Undergraduate in CS or Software Engineering. Proficiency in JavaScript/TypeScript and SQL. Experience with Git, Docker, and agile development workflows.

title: iOS Developer Intern

qualifications: Undergraduate or graduate student in CS. Experience with Swift and SwiftUI. Published App Store projects preferred. Knowledge of UIKit for legacy code maintenance.

title: DevOps Student Engineer
description: Manage Jenkins CI pipelines, write Ansible playbooks for server provisioning, monitor production systems with Prometheus and Grafana, and assist with Docker containerization of research applications.
qualifications: Junior or senior in CS, IT, or Systems Engineering. Linux systems administration experience. Familiarity with CI/CD, containerization, and monitoring tools.

title: Data Science Intern – University Athletics
description: Analyze player performance data using Python and R, build predictive models for game strategy, create dashboards in Tableau for coaching staff, and process video tracking data.
qualifications: Undergraduate or graduate student in Data Science, Statistics, or CS. Experience with sports analytics preferred. Strong data visualization skills.

title: Network Operations Student Technician

title: Accessibility Testing Student Worker

qualifications: Undergraduate in CS, IT, or Informatics. Knowledge of HTML, CSS, and ARIA attributes. Familiarity with assistive technology and accessibility standards.

title: Student Nurse Extern – Psychiatric/Behavioral Health Unit
description: Assist psychiatric RNs with patient assessments, therapeutic communication, safety rounds, medication administration, group therapy facilitation, and de-escalation interventions.
qualifications: BSN student who has completed Psychiatric Nursing clinical rotation. CPI certification preferred. Strong therapeutic communication skills.

title: Student Nurse Extern – Operating Room
description: Assist circulating and scrub nurses in the OR, maintain sterile fields, perform instrument counts, position patients, document intraoperative events, and observe various surgical procedures.
qualifications: BSN student in final year. Completed perioperative nursing elective or clinical. Ability to stand for extended periods. Calm under high-stress situations.

title: Student Nurse Extern – Neonatal Intensive Care Unit
description: Provide care to premature and critically ill newborns under RN supervision including feeding, bathing, vital sign monitoring, kangaroo care education for parents, and charting in Epic.
qualifications: BSN student with completed OB and Pediatric clinicals. NRP certification required. Gentle demeanor and comfort with fragile patient population.

title: Speech-Language Pathology Graduate Clinician

qualifications: Graduate student in Speech-Language Pathology. Completed academic prerequisites for clinical practicum. Strong clinical writing skills.

title: Outdoor Recreation Trip Leader

title: Multicultural Center Student Coordinator

title: Student Government Senator (Paid Position)

title: Campus Mail Services Student Worker
description: Sort and distribute incoming mail and packages, notify students of package arrivals, operate postage meters, process outgoing departmental mail, and assist with end-of-semester package surplus.
qualifications: Enrolled undergraduate. Ability to lift packages up to 40 lbs. Detail-oriented and trustworthy with confidential correspondence. Morning availability preferred.

title: Planetarium Show Presenter

institution: Stanford University Office of Technology Licensing

title: Student Emergency Medical Technician (EMT)

qualifications: Undergraduate with current NREMT-B certification and state EMT license. Must complete agency-specific orientation. Clean driving record. Available for 12-hour shifts.

qualifications: Undergraduate in Meteorology. Completed synoptic meteorology courses. Familiarity with GR2Analyst and AWIPS. Passion for weather forecasting.

title: Cadaver Lab Prosector

qualifications: Graduate student in Anatomy, Medical Science, or related field. Prior cadaver lab experience required. Detailed knowledge of human anatomy. Must be comfortable with extended dissection work.

title: Grant Writing Student Assistant – Office of Research

qualifications: Graduate student in any field. Excellent writing and editing skills. Familiarity with grants.gov and eRA Commons preferred. Detail-oriented with ability to manage multiple deadlines.

title: Student Interpreter – Deaf and Hard of Hearing Services

title: Vivarium Student Caretaker

qualifications: Undergraduate in Biology or Animal Science. Must complete IACUC animal handling training. Comfortable working with rodents. Weekend availability required.

qualifications: Undergraduate or community member, age 18+. Strong acting and memorization abilities. Comfortable with physical examinations in an educational context. Training provided.

title: Herbarium Student Curator

qualifications: Undergraduate in Botany, Biology, or Environmental Science. Plant identification skills preferred. Careful hand coordination for specimen mounting. Patience for detail work.

title: Science Communication Fellow (Student Position)
institution: American Association for the Advancement of Science / Georgetown University
description: Write lay-audience summaries of research papers, produce social media content about scientific breakthroughs, interview researchers for podcast segments, and assist with science policy briefs.
qualifications: Graduate student in any STEM field with strong writing skills. Published science writing samples preferred. Interest in science communication and policy.

title: Student Archivist – University History Collection
description: Process archival collections by arranging and describing materials, create finding aids in ArchivesSpace, digitize fragile documents, and assist researchers in the reading room.
qualifications: Undergraduate or graduate student in History, Library Science, or related field. Familiarity with archival standards (DACS). Careful handling of fragile materials.

title: Peer Tutor – Organic Chemistry

qualifications: Completed Organic Chemistry I and II with an A. Patient and skilled at breaking down complex mechanisms. Tutoring certification through CRLA preferred.

title: Graduate Student Instructor – Public Speaking
description: Serve as instructor of record for a section of COMM 200 (Introduction to Public Speaking). Design lesson plans, evaluate student speeches using rubrics, provide constructive feedback, and submit grades.
qualifications: Graduate student in Communication Studies. Prior teaching or TA experience required. Strong public speaking skills and familiarity with rhetorical theory.

title: Student Athletic Trainer
description: Assist certified athletic trainers with injury evaluation, taping and bracing, rehabilitation exercises, and game-day medical coverage for varsity and club sports teams.
qualifications: Undergraduate in Athletic Training or Exercise Science. CPR/AED certified. Enrolled in or completed Athletic Training clinical coursework.

title: Teaching Assistant – Introduction to Computer Networks
description: Grade Wireshark labs and socket programming assignments, hold office hours, manage the course discussion board, and develop supplemental materials on TCP/IP, routing, and DNS.
qualifications: Graduate student in CS or ECE. Completed Computer Networks with an A. Proficiency in Python and C. Familiarity with network simulation tools like Mininet.

title: Student Greenhouse Manager – Botany Department
description: Oversee daily greenhouse operations, supervise 3 student workers, manage watering schedules, order supplies, coordinate with researchers on space allocation, and maintain climate control systems.
qualifications: Graduate student in Botany or Horticulture. Supervisory experience preferred. Knowledge of plant pathology and integrated pest management.

title: Research Assistant – Human-Robot Interaction
institution: University of Southern California Interaction Lab
description: Design and conduct user studies evaluating social robot behaviors, program robot expressions using Python and ROS, analyze interaction data with mixed-methods approaches, and co-author publications.
qualifications: Graduate student in CS, Psychology, or HCI. Experience with Python and ROS. IRB protocol experience preferred. Interest in social robotics.

title: Teaching Assistant – Intro to African American Studies
description: Lead weekly discussion sections covering themes in Black history, culture, and politics. Grade analytical essays, curate multimedia resources for lectures, and mentor students on research projects.
qualifications: Graduate student in African American Studies, History, Sociology, or related field. Strong knowledge of the Black intellectual tradition. Facilitation experience with diverse groups.

title: Student Aquatics Director – Campus Pool
description: Supervise lifeguard staff, schedule shifts, conduct in-service training drills, manage pool chemical testing logs, coordinate swim lesson programs, and serve as head lifeguard during events.
qualifications: Undergraduate with current Lifeguard Instructor and Water Safety Instructor certifications. At least one year of lifeguarding experience. CPO certification preferred.

title: Computer Science Grader
description: Grade student code submissions for CS 2110 (Computer Organization) using automated and manual review. Provide written feedback on coding style, correctness, and efficiency. Attend weekly calibration meetings.
qualifications: Undergraduate who completed CS 2110 with an A. Proficiency in C and assembly language. Comfortable reading and evaluating others' code.

title: Teaching Assistant – Biostatistics
institution: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill

qualifications: Graduate student in Biostatistics or Statistics. Proficiency in SAS and R required. Ability to clearly explain statistical concepts to non-statisticians.

title: Research Assistant – Digital Humanities
description: Build interactive web visualizations of historical text corpora using D3.js, perform text mining and topic modeling in Python, manage project GitHub repositories, and contribute to conference papers.
qualifications: Graduate student in Computer Science, English, or Digital Humanities. Experience with Python (NLTK, spaCy) and JavaScript. Interest in computational literary analysis.

title: Student Peer Financial Counselor
description: Conduct one-on-one financial literacy sessions with students, help create personal budgets, explain student loan repayment options, lead workshops on credit management, and track client outcomes.
qualifications: Undergraduate junior or senior in Finance, Business, or Economics. Completed financial counselor training. Non-judgmental approach to discussing money with peers.

title: Veterinary Technician Student Worker
institution: University of Florida College of Veterinary Medicine
description: Assist veterinarians with small animal examinations, restrain patients for procedures, prepare surgical packs, monitor anesthesia recovery, and maintain treatment area cleanliness.
qualifications: Undergraduate in Animal Science or Pre-Veterinary track. Animal handling experience required. Comfortable with cats, dogs, and exotic species. Rabies vaccination required.

title: Student Ambassador – College of Engineering
description: Lead prospective engineering student tours, present at recruitment events and open houses, share personal experiences in panel discussions, and assist admissions staff with outreach communications.
qualifications: Sophomore or above in any engineering major with a 3.0+ GPA. Excellent public speaking skills. Enthusiasm for representing the College of Engineering.

title: Research Assistant – Bioinformatics and Proteomics
description: Analyze mass spectrometry data using MaxQuant and Perseus, build protein interaction networks, write Python scripts for data pipeline automation, and present findings at lab meetings.
qualifications: Graduate student in Bioinformatics, Biochemistry, or CS. Experience with proteomics data analysis and Python scripting. Knowledge of protein databases (UniProt, STRING).

title: Student Graphic Novelist / Cartoonist – Campus Magazine
description: Create weekly comic strips for the campus arts magazine, illustrate editorial content, collaborate with writers on visual storytelling projects, and participate in layout and design meetings.
qualifications: Undergraduate in Illustration, Cartooning, or Visual Arts. Published comics or a strong portfolio. Proficiency in Procreate or Clip Studio Paint. Ability to meet weekly deadlines.

title: Teaching Assistant – Human-Computer Interaction
description: Grade design projects and heuristic evaluation reports, lead studio sessions on prototyping and user testing, hold office hours, and help coordinate guest speaker logistics.
qualifications: Graduate student in HCI, Informatics, or CS. Experience with Figma and user research methods. Strong design critique skills.

title: Student Greenhouse Pest Management Scout
description: Monitor greenhouse crops for pest and disease outbreaks, set and evaluate sticky traps, identify insects using microscopy, record scouting data, and assist with integrated pest management treatments.
qualifications: Undergraduate in Entomology, Plant Pathology, or Horticulture. Basic insect identification skills. Willingness to work in hot greenhouse conditions.

title: Student UI/UX Designer – University Mobile App
description: Design wireframes and high-fidelity mockups in Figma for the campus mobile app, conduct user interviews and usability tests, create design system components, and present recommendations to stakeholders.
qualifications: Undergraduate or graduate student in Industrial Design, HCI, or CS. Strong Figma portfolio required. Understanding of iOS and Material Design guidelines.

title: Student Barista – Campus Coffee Shop
description: Prepare espresso drinks, drip coffee, and specialty beverages. Operate POS registers, maintain food safety standards, manage pastry display, stock supplies, and provide friendly customer service.
qualifications: Currently enrolled undergraduate. Prior barista or food service experience preferred but not required. ServSafe food handler certification within 30 days of hire.
